answers.append({"task_id":"T01c","answer":"RAM (Random Access Memory) is volatile, read-write memory used to hold data and programs the CPU is actively working on while the computer is powered on. answers.append({"task_id":"T06","answer":"Bug: the function returns the largest element (nums[-1]) instead of the second largest. Corrected function:\ndef second_largest(nums):\n    nums = list(set(nums))\n    nums.sort()\n    return nums[-2]"})
answers.append({"task_id":"T06b","answer":"Bug: strings have no .reverse() method (it returns None and lists are needed), and the comparison is reversed. Corrected function:\ndef is_palindrome(s):\n    return s == s[::-1]"})
# T07b logic
from itertools import permutations
people = ['Emma','Liam','Priya']
pets = ['cat','dog','parrot']
sols=[]
for p in permutations(pets):
    E,L,Pr = p
    if L in ('cat','dog','hamster','rabbit'):  # Liam allergic to fur -> not cat/dog; parrot is fine
        continue
    if E == 'parrot':
        continue
    if Pr == 'dog':
        continue
    sols.append((E,L,Pr))
# Only one solution expected
ans = sols[0]
mapping = {'Emma':ans[0],'Liam':ans[1],'Priya':ans[2]}
text = "Emma owns the "+mapping['Emma']+", Liam owns the "+mapping['Liam']+", and Priya owns the "+mapping['Priya']+"."
answers.append({"task_id":"T07b","answer":text})

submit(answers=answers)
